mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k indicates strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns fail to exceed 1.5% in 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k, or Sharpe ratio < 0.5 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1.6 million units signal improved consumer confidence and business investment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in HOUST > 1600 periods show negative or flat performance (return < 0.5%) in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) below 102.5 suggests supply chain disruptions, increasing inflationary pressures and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in INDPRO < 102.5 periods exceed 1.2% in 50% of cases or exhibit negative correlation with INDPRO changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate deflationary risks, prompting central bank intervention and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in GDPC1/CPIAUCSL decline periods show negative performance (return < -0.8%) in > 35% of cases or exhibit zero correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4% combined with falling payrolls (PAYEMS) signals economic contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in contractionary regimes exceed -1.5% in < 25% of cases or show positive correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle marked by rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and declining RSAFS signals reduced liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in tightening regimes exceed -1.2% in < 30% of cases or show positive correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.5 combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% indicates overheating supply-side dynamics, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns in overheating regimes exceed 0.7% in > 40% of cases or show negative correlation with the composite signal.
